chunk_summary: Guidelines for assessing and supporting breastfeeding in mother–infant dyads under six months with moderate acute malnutrition, emphasizing supplementation options, follow-up intensity, and escalation criteria.
learning_objectives: Understand the dyad assessment process for infants under six months with MAM.
Learn appropriate supplementation methods that preserve breastfeeding.
Identify follow-up intensity and escalation criteria for facility-based care.
Recognize risks associated with inappropriate feeding practices like diluted formula or animal milk.
key_facts: The mother–infant dyad is the focus of care for infants under six months with MAM.
Expressed breast milk is the first choice for supplementation; infant formula is used if breast milk is unavailable.
Follow-up intensity includes checks at 48–72 hours, 7 days, and weekly until consistent weight gain is achieved.
Avoid animal milk or thin cereal gruels due to infection risk and low nutrient density.
Escalate to facility-based care for danger signs like dehydration, hypothermia, oedema, or lack of weight gain.
reference_quote: Mothers’ Absolute Affection (MAA): operational guidelines emphasize skilled lactation support and breastfeeding preservation in malnutrition management. (Source: https://nhm.gov.in/MAA/Operational_Guidelines.pdf)
document_key_topics: Dyad assessment for mother–infant pairs under six months
Follow-up intensity and escalation criteria for MAM
